facts.statement: The client hired Trellis Studio to build a website for her bakery.
facts.source_quote: I hired a company called Trellis Studio to build a website for my bakery

facts.statement: The agreed price was $4,000, with a $2,000 deposit paid up front.
facts.source_quote: We agreed they would deliver the finished website for four thousand dollars, and I paid two thousand dollars up front as a deposit.

facts.statement: Trellis Studio missed the delivery deadline and stopped responding.
facts.source_quote: They were supposed to have it done by last Friday, but they just stopped responding to me.

facts.statement: The agreement was signed in writing, by email, on March 8th.
facts.source_quote: we did sign a short contract by email on March 8th

facts.statement: A witness — the client's business partner Dana — was present for the agreement.
facts.source_quote: my business partner Dana was there the whole time and can confirm what was promised.

facts.statement: The client tried to resolve the matter before seeking counsel.
facts.source_quote: I emailed them twice and left a voicemail asking them to finish the site or refund me, but they never replied.

facts.statement: The client has the email correspondence but cannot locate the deposit invoice.
facts.source_quote: I've got all our emails saved, but I cannot find the invoice for my deposit anywhere.

facts.statement: The client lost the $2,000 deposit and expects added cost to finish the website.
facts.source_quote: That two thousand dollars is just gone, and now I'll have to pay someone else to finish the site.

prompts: Ask Dana to provide a short written statement of what was promised.
Request a copy of the signed contract and the deposit invoice.
